body_txt: As you know, Growing Up Sucks . Some children, however, have found a way to halt their aging at childhood via Applied Phlebotinum or some supernatural means. Most of the time they'll have the mind, emotional maturity, and/or sensibilities of a child as well as a prepubescent body; adults in children's bodies are more likely to find it inconvenient or downright sucky, which is the Sub-Trope , Not Growing Up Sucks . Note that this must happen In-Universe to count. If characters in a long runner series stay forever at a given age simply to keep the setting the way it is (like The Simpsons all being the same age for any time longer than a period of say, roughly three to five years despite how differences, such as growth of children, would be noticeable by then), that's Comic-Book Time . Older Than They Look is the Super-Trope of this. Compare Not Allowed to Grow Up , and Really Seven Hundred Years Old . Compare also with Immortality Inducer , which tends to include "anti-aging" as part of the package. Contrast Rapid Aging . Not to Be Confused with One of the Kids . Do not confuse with Manchild .
